title: Modern Platform Bed Frame (Dowel-Leg, All Sizes)
summary: A 2x6 box-frame platform bed on thick round dowel legs, with 45°-cornered rails and a center support keyed to your mattress size — five complete, distinct cut lists, twin through California king.
description:
A close cousin of the hairpin-leg platform bed elsewhere in this catalog, built the same way — a 2x6 box frame with 2x2 cleats to carry the slats, and a center support running the bed's length — but standing on thick (2"+ diameter) round wood dowel legs instead of steel hairpins. The dowel legs read heavier and more traditional; the hairpin version reads more mid-century. Pick whichever fits the room, since the frame underneath is otherwise the same idea.

The angled corner pieces are the one detail worth slowing down for: they're cut at 45° from scrap 2x6, and it's safer to cut these small angled pieces from a longer board clamped to the saw deck than to try to hold a short offcut steady by hand near the blade. **Each size below is a genuinely separate cut list**, not one frame scaled by a formula — larger sizes get more center-support legs (one for twin, two for full/queen, three for king and California king) because a wider bed needs more mid-span backup or the slats sag under a mattress. Build only the size you need.

tools.note: For the 45° angled corner pieces — cut from a longer offcut, not a short piece held by hand.

materials.name: Pine, 2x6, 8 ft
materials.note: Quantity for full/queen/king sizes — twin needs only 3.

materials.name: Pine, 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Center support and center legs — quantity varies by size, up to 3 for king/California king.

materials.name: Round wood dowel legs, 2" diameter or larger
materials.note: Cut to 7-1/4" for the corner legs and center legs alike.

materials.name: Pine, 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Mattress slats — quantity scales with bed width.

materials.name: Lag screws, 4-1/2" or longer
materials.note: Attaches the leg assemblies to the bed frame.

cutList.note: Full/queen use 80"; king/Cal king use 80"/84" respectively.
Match to side rail length for the size being built.
Both ends cut at 45°, ends not parallel — long point to long point.
Full/queen/king/Cal king need 2, matched to bed length.

steps.title: Attach the cleats to the side rails
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the side rails, positioned within 1" of the top or bottom edge so the cleat you're about to attach doesn't cover them. Glue and screw the 2x2 cleats to the inside face of each side rail with 2-1/2" screws — about one every 8–12". Build two identical side rails.

steps.title: Attach the top and bottom rails
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom rails between the two side rails with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue, forming the outer box. Work upside down — it's easier to see the joints this way.

steps.title: Cut and install the angled corner braces
steps.body: Set the miter saw to 45° and cut the angled corner braces from a longer scrap 2x6 — cut the small piece off the end of a longer board rather than trying to hold a short offcut steady near the blade. Drill pocket holes as marked, predrill for the leg lag screws, and install a corner leg into each brace with 4" lag screws and glue.
materials: Round wood dowel legs, 2" diameter or larger

steps.title: Install the corner leg assemblies
steps.body: Fit the corner leg assemblies inside the bed frame at each corner and attach through the pocket holes. Add extra 2-1/2" screws through the small angled ends into the 2x6 frame for a stronger joint.

steps.title: Build and install the center support
steps.body: Cut the center support(s) to length and drill two pocket holes on each end. Attach a center leg to each end with 2-1/2" screws and glue, then place the assembly inside the bed frame and attach through the pocket holes to the top and bottom rails.

steps.title: Lay and attach the slats
steps.body:
Lay the slats across the frame with about a 4" gap between them for most standard mattresses — check your own mattress's support requirements, since some need tighter spacing. Attach each slat with the shorter self-tapping screws.

Sand every surface before moving the frame into the bedroom.

images.alt: Modern platform bed frame with round dowel legs
